sections.paragraph:
ἐλευθέριος, (α), ον (since Theognis 538; Democr. 282 Diels; Pla.; EpArist 246; Philo, Joseph.) appropriate for a free pers. ἐπιθήσει σοι ἐ. στέφανον. he (Christ) will place on you a freedom wreath (cp. Simonides 94a, 2; b, 2 Diehl2 στέφανος ἐλευθερίας) AcPl Ha 2, 31.—DELG s.v. ἐλεύθερος.

Theognis Theognis , VI b.c.—List 5

Democr Democr , phil., V/IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Diehl Diehl , ed., s. AnthLG—List 5

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 316
